mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep technical expertise in AI. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and potential resource requirements for running the application effectively.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local environment to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to access the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Flowise to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace flowise

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Flowise deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This will remove the Flowise application.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods --namespace flowise

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade Flowise to the specified version.
> Verify that the Flowise pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Flowise deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Flowise service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get svc --namespace flowise' and ensure it's correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new visual components for AI agents, improved API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configuration files to use the new authentication methods.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:3000 with all components functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
